title: Baloghia inophylla
intro: Baloghia inophylla, also known as Baloghia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of New Caledonia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Baloghia inophylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments in folk medicine.
